messages.content: перефразуй: Great, everyone. At home, you were working on Exercise 6 in your Student Book. You had to describe the type of clothing you would wear in various situations. Let’s imagine that you need to go to school.

Excellent work, all of you. While you were at your homes, your task was to tackle Exercise 6 from your Student Book. This involved outlining the kinds of outfits you'd select for different scenarios. Now, envision that your next destination is school.

перефразуй попроще: Great, everyone. At home, you were working on Exercise 6 in your Student Book. You had to describe the type of clothing you would wear in various situations. Let’s imagine that you need to go to school.

Good job, everyone. You were doing Exercise 6 from your books at home. You were writing about what clothes you'd wear for different things. Now, think about what you would wear if you were going to school.
перефразуй попроще: You've shared some very cool ideas of what to wear for different occasions. Don't forget to use the new words we learnt to talk about your style.
You've come up with some great suggestions for what to wear on different occasions. Remember to use the new words we learned when you talk about your style.
перефразуй попроще: Let's talk a little more, you should have done exercise 8 at home. You had to choose the person most different from you and describe them in detail.
Let's chat a bit more. You were supposed to do Exercise 8 at home. It was about picking the person who is the most unlike you and describing them closely.
перефразуй попроще: Great, so tell me which active vocabulary you used in your description?
Alright, can you tell me which new words you used when you described them?
перефразуй попроще: Great/good/ u r right/are you shure?/does everyone agree? /who can help student 1, 2, 3…?
Nice! Okay, so you think that's correct? Is everyone okay with that? Who can give a hand to Student 1, 2, 3, and so on?
перефразуй попроще: And your last homework assignment for today was ex 9 you had to do Exercise 1c in the Word Store using the underlined words and expressions in the quiz.
For your last piece of homework today, you needed to complete Exercise 9. This was Exercise 1c in the Word Store where you had to use the words and phrases that were underlined in the quiz.
перефразуй попроще: Now let's move on to your workbook. please look at the screen (children do not have a textbook).
Okay, let's switch to your workbook now. Everyone please watch the screen (since you don't have the textbook with you).

перефразуй попроще: Okay, we have a list of clothes, accessories and some characteristics and three pictures of people. We need to list what we see in the picture with Emma, Meg, and Ryan. Some words might be in several photos. Also, there is one extra word in the list.

Alright, we've got clothes, things you wear, and some words about how things look. We also have three photos of Emma, Meg, and Ryan. We need to write down what we notice in each person's picture. Some words might show up in more than one photo. Plus, there's one word in the list we don't need.
перефразуй попроще: So the first word is belt. Guys please send in the chat the number of the picture in which you see this item
The first word we're looking at is "belt." Everyone, please type in the chat which photo number you see the belt in.

student 1 Is there a belt in any of the pictures? in which picture? what do others think? do you agree with student 1? I don't think there's a belt.  let's leave this word for now and come back at the end if we find something else extra in this list

Student 1, can you spot a belt in any of the pictures? Which one? What does everyone else think? Do you all agree with Student 1? I personally don't see a belt. Let's skip this word for the moment and we'll return to it at the end to see if it's the extra word from the list.
накидай мені дежурні відповіді вчителя на англійській мові. що завдання виконав гарно. або ще подума. або хто допоможе

Sure, here are some common teacher responses in English:

1. "Well done on completing the assignment. You've done a good job."
2. "Nice effort, but let's think about this a bit more."
3. "Who can offer some help here?"

Some more variations could include:

4. "Great work on this task. It looks like you really understood it."
5. "You're on the right track, but try to give it another thought."
6. "Can anyone assist with this question?"

And a few more:

7. "Excellent work! You've nailed the assignment."
8. "Almost there, maybe consider a different approach?"
9. "Does anyone have ideas they can share to help out?"
